data.reasoningText:
Let me understand the project structure. It's a .NET 10 console app with:
- Microsoft.Agents.AI
- Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI
- OpenAI

I need to build an AI agent that:
1. Has a web search tool
2. Has a note-taking tool
3. Can research a topic and summarize findings

Let me check what .NET SDK is available and what packages are accessible.

Since this uses `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (the new AI abstraction in .NET), I should use its tool-calling capabilities. The `Microsoft.Agents.AI` package likely provides agentic capabilities.


I'll start by checking the available .NET SDK version and exploring how these packages work together.

arguments.name: explore-ai-apis
arguments.prompt:
I need to understand the APIs available in these .NET NuGet packages to build an AI agent with tool calling:

1. Microsoft.Extensions.AI (v9.10.2) - at /home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai/9.10.2/
2. Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions (v9.10.2) - at /home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.abstractions/9.10.2/
3. Microsoft.Agents.AI (v1.3.0) - at /home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.3.0/
4. Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI (v9.*) - at /home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.openai/

For each package, look at the XML docs (*.xml files) in the lib/ directories and README files. I need to understand:
- How to create an IChatClient
- How to define tools (AIFunction / AITool)  
- How the agent loop works (does it auto-invoke tools or do I need manual loop?)
- How to use ChatOptions with tools
- Key classes and their namespaces

Decompile or examine the DLLs if needed. Also check if there are any README.md or docs in the package directories.

Focus especially on: FunctionInvokingChatClient, ChatClientBuilder, AIFunctionFactory, McpToolsAIFunction, or similar tool-related classes.

result.content:
1. # Microsoft.Extensions.AI
2. 
3. .NET developers need to integrate and interact with a growing variety of artificial intelligence (AI) services in their apps. The `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` libraries provide a unified approach for representing generative AI components, and enable seamless integration and interoperability with various AI services.
4. 
5. ## The packages
6. 
7. The [Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions) package provides the core exchange types, including [`IChatClient`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.extensions.ai.ichatclient) and [`IEmbeddingGenerator<TInput,TEmbedding>`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.extensions.ai.iembeddinggenerator-2). Any .NET library that provides an LLM client can implement the `IChatClient` interface to enable seamless integration with consuming code.
8. 
9. The [Microsoft.Extensions.AI](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Extensions.AI) package has an implicit dependency on the `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions` package. This package enables you to easily integrate components such as automatic function tool invocation, telemetry, and caching into your applications using familiar dependency injection and middleware patterns. For example, it provides the [`UseOpenTelemetry(ChatClientBuilder, ILoggerFactory, String, Action<OpenTelemetryChatClient>)`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.extensions.ai.opentelemetrychatclientbuilderextensions.useopentelemetry#microsoft-extensions-ai-opentelemetrychatclientbuilderextensions-useopentelemetry(microsoft-extensions-ai-chatclientbuilder-microsoft-extensions-logging-iloggerfactory-system-string-system-action((microsoft-extensions-ai-opentelemetrychatclient)))) extension method, which adds OpenTelemetry support to the chat client pipeline.
10. 
11. ## Which package to reference
12. 
13. Libraries that provide implementations of the abstractions typically reference only `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`.
14. 
15. To also have access to higher-level utilities for working with generative AI components, reference the `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` package instead (which itself references `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`). Most consuming applications and services should reference the `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` package along with one or more libraries that provide concrete implementations of the abstractions.
16. 
17. ## Install the package
18. 
19. From the command-line:
20. 
21. ```console
22. dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.AI
23. ```
24. 
25. Or directly in the C# project file:
26. 
27. ```xml
28. <ItemGroup>
29.   <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI" Version="[CURRENTVERSION]" />
30. </ItemGroup>
31. ```
32. 
33. ## Documentation
34. 
35. Refer to the [Microsoft.Extensions.AI libraries documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai) for more information and API usage examples.
36. 
37. ## Feedback & Contributing
38. 
39. We welcome feedback and contributions in [our GitHub repo](https://github.com/dotnet/extensions).
40.

index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai/9.10.2/README.md
+++ b/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai/9.10.2/README.md
@@ -1,40 +1,40 @@
 # Microsoft.Extensions.AI
 
 .NET developers need to integrate and interact with a growing variety of artificial intelligence (AI) services in their apps. The `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` libraries provide a unified approach for representing generative AI components, and enable seamless integration and interoperability with various AI services.
 
 ## The packages
 
 The [Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions) package provides the core exchange types, including [`IChatClient`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.extensions.ai.ichatclient) and [`IEmbeddingGenerator<TInput,TEmbedding>`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.extensions.ai.iembeddinggenerator-2). Any .NET library that provides an LLM client can implement the `IChatClient` interface to enable seamless integration with consuming code.
 
 The [Microsoft.Extensions.AI](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Extensions.AI) package has an implicit dependency on the `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions` package. This package enables you to easily integrate components such as automatic function tool invocation, telemetry, and caching into your applications using familiar dependency injection and middleware patterns. For example, it provides the [`UseOpenTelemetry(ChatClientBuilder, ILoggerFactory, String, Action<OpenTelemetryChatClient>)`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.extensions.ai.opentelemetrychatclientbuilderextensions.useopentelemetry#microsoft-extensions-ai-opentelemetrychatclientbuilderextensions-useopentelemetry(microsoft-extensions-ai-chatclientbuilder-microsoft-extensions-logging-iloggerfactory-system-string-system-action((microsoft-extensions-ai-opentelemetrychatclient)))) extension method, which adds OpenTelemetry support to the chat client pipeline.
 
 ## Which package to reference
 
 Libraries that provide implementations of the abstractions typically reference only `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`.
 
 To also have access to higher-level utilities for working with generative AI components, reference the `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` package instead (which itself references `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`). Most consuming applications and services should reference the `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` package along with one or more libraries that provide concrete implementations of the abstractions.
 
 ## Install the package
 
 From the command-line:
 
 ```console
 dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.AI
 ```
 
 Or directly in the C# project file:
 
 ```xml
 <ItemGroup>
   <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI" Version="[CURRENTVERSION]" />
 </ItemGroup>
 ```
 
 ## Documentation
 
 Refer to the [Microsoft.Extensions.AI libraries documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai) for more information and API usage examples.
 
 ## Feedback & Contributing
 
 We welcome feedback and contributions in [our GitHub repo](https://github.com/dotnet/extensions).

1. # About Microsoft Agent Framework
2. 
3. Microsoft Agent Framework is a comprehensive .NET library for building, orchestrating, and deploying AI agents and multi-agent workflows. The framework provides everything from simple chat agents to complex multi-agent systems with graph-based orchestration capabilities.
4. 
5. ## Key Features
6. 
7. - **Multi-Agent Orchestration**: Coordinate multiple agents using sequential, concurrent, group chat, and handoff patterns
8. - **Graph-based Workflows**: Connect agents and functions with streaming, checkpointing, and human-in-the-loop capabilities, with both imperative or declarative workflow support
9. - **Multiple Provider Support**: Seamlessly integrate with various LLM providers with more being added continuously
10. - **Extensible Middleware**: Flexible request/response processing with custom pipelines and exception handling
11. - **Built-in Observability**: OpenTelemetry integration for distributed tracing, monitoring, and debugging
12. - **Cross-Platform**: Compatible with .NET 8.0, .NET Standard 2.0, and .NET Framework for broad deployment options
13. 
14. Whether you're building simple AI assistants or complex multi-agent systems, Microsoft Agent Framework provides the tools and abstractions needed to create robust, scalable AI applications in .NET.
15. 
16. # Getting Started ⚡
17. 
18. - Learn more at the [documentation site](https://learn.microsoft.com/agent-framework/overview/agent-framework-overview).
19. - Join the [Discord community](https://discord.gg/b5zjErwbQM).
20. - Follow the team on [Semantic Kernel blog](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/semantic-kernel/).
21. - Check out the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/microsoft/agent-framework) for the latest updates.
22.

@@ -1,22 +1,22 @@
 # About Microsoft Agent Framework
 
 Microsoft Agent Framework is a comprehensive .NET library for building, orchestrating, and deploying AI agents and multi-agent workflows. The framework provides everything from simple chat agents to complex multi-agent systems with graph-based orchestration capabilities.
 
 ## Key Features
 
 - **Multi-Agent Orchestration**: Coordinate multiple agents using sequential, concurrent, group chat, and handoff patterns
 - **Graph-based Workflows**: Connect agents and functions with streaming, checkpointing, and human-in-the-loop capabilities, with both imperative or declarative workflow support
 - **Multiple Provider Support**: Seamlessly integrate with various LLM providers with more being added continuously
 - **Extensible Middleware**: Flexible request/response processing with custom pipelines and exception handling
 - **Built-in Observability**: OpenTelemetry integration for distributed tracing, monitoring, and debugging
 - **Cross-Platform**: Compatible with .NET 8.0, .NET Standard 2.0, and .NET Framework for broad deployment options
 
 Whether you're building simple AI assistants or complex multi-agent systems, Microsoft Agent Framework provides the tools and abstractions needed to create robust, scalable AI applications in .NET.
 
 # Getting Started ⚡
 
 - Learn more at the [documentation site](https://learn.microsoft.com/agent-framework/overview/agent-framework-overview).
 - Join the [Discord community](https://discord.gg/b5zjErwbQM).
 - Follow the team on [Semantic Kernel blog](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/semantic-kernel/).
 - Check out the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/microsoft/agent-framework) for the latest updates.

When this client receives a in a chat response from its inner , it responds by invoking the corresponding defined in (or in ), producing a that it sends back to the inner client. This loop is repeated until there are no more function calls to make, or until another stop condition is met, such as hitting . If a requested function is an but not an , the will not attempt to invoke it, and instead allow that to pass back out to the caller. It is then that caller's responsibility to create the appropriate for that call and send it back as part of a subsequent request. Further, if a requested function is an , the will not attempt to invoke it directly. Instead, it will replace that with a that wraps the and indicates that the function requires approval before it can be invoked. The caller is then responsible for responding to that approval request by sending a corresponding in a subsequent request. The will then process that approval response and invoke the function as appropriate. Due to the nature of interactions with an underlying , if any is received for a function that requires approval, all received in that same response will also require approval, even if they were not instances. If this is a concern, consider requesting that multiple tool call requests not be made in a single response, by setting to . <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvocationContext">
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient">
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.FunctionInvocationResult">
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.FunctionInvocationStatus">
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClientBuilderExtensions">
=== FunctionInvokingChatClient ===
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient">
            <summary>
            A delegating chat client that invokes functions defined on <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/>.
            Include this in a chat pipeline to resolve function calls automatically.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            When this client receives a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> in a chat response from its inner
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>, it responds by invoking the corresponding <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> defined
            in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools"/> (or in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools"/>), producing a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent"/>
            that it sends back to the inner client. This loop is repeated until there are no more function calls to make, or until
            another stop condition is met, such as hitting <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.MaximumIterationsPerRequest"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            If a requested function is an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration"/> but not an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>, the
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will not attempt to invoke it, and instead allow that <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/>
            to pass back out to the caller. It is then that caller's responsibility to create the appropriate <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent"/>
            for that call and send it back as part of a subsequent request.
            </para>
            <para>
            Further, if a requested function is an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ApprovalRequiredAIFunction"/>, the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will not
<exited with exit code 0>

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
<member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory">
            <summary>Provides factory methods for creating commonly-used implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</summary>
            <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/quickstarts/use-function-calling">Invoke .NET functions using an AI model.</related>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory._defaultOptions">
            <summary>Holds the default options instance used when creating function.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory.Create(System.Delegate,Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions)">
            <summary>Creates an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instance for a method, specified via a delegate.</summary>
            <param name="method">The method to be represented via the created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</param>
            <param name="options">Metadata to use to override defaults inferred from <paramref name="method"/>.</param>
            <returns>The created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> for invoking <paramref name="method"/>.</returns>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            By default, any parameters to <paramref name="method"/> are sourced from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>'s dictionary
            of key/value pairs and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>'s
            <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>. There are a few exceptions to this:
            <list type="bullet">
              <item>
                <description>
                  <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to the <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> passed into
                  the invocation via <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>'s <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameter. The parameter is
                  not included in the generated JSON schema. The behavior of <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters can't be overridden.
                </description>
              </item>
              <item>
                <description>
                  By default, <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> property
                  and are not included in the JSON schema. If the parameter is optional, such that a default value is provided,
                  <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> is allowed to be <see langword="null"/>; otherwise, <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/>
                  must be non-<see langword="null"/>, or else the invocation will fail with an exception due to the required nature of the parameter.
<exited with exit code 0>

1755.         <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient">
1756.             <summary>Represents a chat client.</summary>
1757.             <remarks>
1758.             <para>
1759.             Applications must consider risks such as prompt injection attacks, data sizes, and the number of messages
1760.             sent to the underlying provider or returned from it. Unless a specific <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> implementation
1761.             explicitly documents safeguards for these concerns, the application is expected to implement appropriate protections.
1762.             </para>
1763.             <para>
1764.             Unless otherwise specified, all members of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> are thread-safe for concurrent use.
1765.             It is expected that all implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> support being used by multiple requests concurrently.
1766.             Instances must not be disposed of while the instance is still in use.
1767.             </para>
1768.             <para>
1769.             However, implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> might mutate the arguments supplied to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and
1770.             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>, such as by configuring the options instance. Thus, consumers of the interface either
1771.             should avoid using shared instances of these arguments for concurrent invocations or should otherwise ensure by construction
1772.             that no <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> instances are used which might employ such mutation. For example, the ConfigureOptions method is
1773.             provided with a callback that could mutate the supplied options argument, and that should be avoided if using a singleton options instance.
1774.             </para>
1775.             </remarks>
1776.             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/quickstarts/build-chat-app">Build an AI chat app with .NET.</related>
1777.             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#the-ichatclient-interface">The IChatClient interface.</related>
1778.         </member>
1779.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
1780.             <summary>Sends chat messages and returns the response.</summary>
1781.             <param name="messages">The sequence of chat messages to send.</param>
1782.             <param name="options">The chat options with which to configure the request.</param>
1783.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
1784.             <returns>The response messages generated by the client.</returns>
1785.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="messages"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
1786.             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#request-a-chat-response">Request a chat response.</related>
1787.         </member>
1788.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
1789.             <summary>Sends chat messages and streams the response.</summary>
1790.             <param name="messages">The sequence of chat messages to send.</param>
1791.             <param name="options">The chat options with which to configure the request.</param>
1792.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
1793.             <returns>The response messages generated by the client.</returns>
1794.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="messages"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
1795.             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#request-a-streaming-chat-response">Request a streaming chat response.</related>
1796.         </member>
1797.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetService(System.Type,System.Object)">
1798.             <summary>Asks the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> for an object of the specified type <paramref name="serviceType"/>.</summary>
1799.             <param name="serviceType">The type of object being requested.</param>
1800.             <param name="serviceKey">An optional key that can be used to help identify the target service.</param>
1801.             <returns>The found object, otherwise <see langword="null"/>.</returns>
1802.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="serviceType"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
1803.             <remarks>
1804.             The purpose of this method is to allow for the retrieval of strongly typed services that might be provided by the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>,
1805.             including itself or any services it might be wrapping. For example, to access the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatClientMetadata"/> for the instance,
1806.             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetService(System.Type,System.Object)"/> may be used to request it.
1807.             </remarks>
1808.         </member>

1813.             <remarks>

         <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient">
             <summary>Represents a chat client.</summary>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             Applications must consider risks such as prompt injection attacks, data sizes, and the number of messages
             sent to the underlying provider or returned from it. Unless a specific <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> implementation
             explicitly documents safeguards for these concerns, the application is expected to implement appropriate protections.
             </para>
             <para>
             Unless otherwise specified, all members of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> are thread-safe for concurrent use.
             It is expected that all implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> support being used by multiple requests concurrently.
             Instances must not be disposed of while the instance is still in use.
             </para>
             <para>
             However, implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> might mutate the arguments supplied to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and
             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>, such as by configuring the options instance. Thus, consumers of the interface either
             should avoid using shared instances of these arguments for concurrent invocations or should otherwise ensure by construction
             that no <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> instances are used which might employ such mutation. For example, the ConfigureOptions method is
             provided with a callback that could mutate the supplied options argument, and that should be avoided if using a singleton options instance.
             </para>
             </remarks>
             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/quickstarts/build-chat-app">Build an AI chat app with .NET.</related>
             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#the-ichatclient-interface">The IChatClient interface.</related>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
             <summary>Sends chat messages and returns the response.</summary>
             <param name="messages">The sequence of chat messages to send.</param>
             <param name="options">The chat options with which to configure the request.</param>
             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
             <returns>The response messages generated by the client.</returns>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="messages"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#request-a-chat-response">Request a chat response.</related>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
             <summary>Sends chat messages and streams the response.</summary>
             <param name="messages">The sequence of chat messages to send.</param>
             <param name="options">The chat options with which to configure the request.</param>
             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
             <returns>The response messages generated by the client.</returns>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="messages"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#request-a-streaming-chat-response">Request a streaming chat response.</related>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetService(System.Type,System.Object)">
             <summary>Asks the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> for an object of the specified type <paramref name="serviceType"/>.</summary>
             <param name="serviceType">The type of object being requested.</param>
             <param name="serviceKey">An optional key that can be used to help identify the target service.</param>
             <returns>The found object, otherwise <see langword="null"/>.</returns>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="serviceType"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <remarks>
             The purpose of this method is to allow for the retrieval of strongly typed services that might be provided by the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>,
             including itself or any services it might be wrapping. For example, to access the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatClientMetadata"/> for the instance,
             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetService(System.Type,System.Object)"/> may be used to request it.
             </remarks>
         </member>

1004.         <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions">
1005.             <summary>Represents the options for a chat request.</summary>
1006.             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#provide-options">Provide options.</related>
1007.         </member>
1008.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.#ctor">
1009.             <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/> class.</summary>
1010.         </member>
1011.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions)">
1012.             <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/> class, performing a shallow copy of all properties from <paramref name="other"/>.</summary>
1013.         </member>
1014.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ConversationId">
1015.             <summary>Gets or sets an optional identifier used to associate a request with an existing conversation.</summary>
1016.             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#stateless-vs-stateful-clients">Stateless vs. stateful clients.</related>
1017.         </member>
1018.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Instructions">
1019.             <summary>Gets or sets additional per-request instructions to be provided to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>.</summary>
1020.         </member>
1021.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Temperature">
1022.             <summary>Gets or sets the temperature for generating chat responses.</summary>
1023.             <remarks>
1024.             This value controls the randomness of predictions made by the model. Use a lower value to decrease randomness in the response.
1025.             </remarks>
1026.         </member>
1027.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.MaxOutputTokens">
1028.             <summary>Gets or sets the maximum number of tokens in the generated chat response.</summary>
1029.         </member>
1030.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.TopP">
1031.             <summary>Gets or sets the "nucleus sampling" factor (or "top p") for generating chat responses.</summary>
1032.             <remarks>
1033.             Nucleus sampling is an alternative to sampling with temperature where the model
1034.             considers the results of the tokens with <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.TopP"/> probability mass.
1035.             For example, 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.
1036.             </remarks>
1037.         </member>
1038.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.TopK">
1039.             <summary>
1040.             Gets or sets the number of most probable tokens that the model considers when generating the next part of the text.
1041.             </summary>
1042.             <remarks>
1043.             This property reduces the probability of generating nonsense. A higher value gives more diverse answers, while a lower value is more conservative.
1044.             </remarks>
1045.         </member>
1046.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.FrequencyPenalty">
1047.             <summary>
1048.             Gets or sets the penalty for repeated tokens in chat responses proportional to how many times they've appeared.
1049.             </summary>
1050.             <remarks>
1051.             You can modify this value to reduce the repetitiveness of generated tokens. The higher the value, the stronger a penalty
1052.             is applied to previously present tokens, proportional to how many times they've already appeared in the prompt or prior generation.
1053.             </remarks>
1054.         </member>
1055.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.PresencePenalty">
1056.             <summary>
1057.             Gets or sets a value that influences the probability of generated tokens appearing based on their existing presence in generated text.
1058.             </summary>
1059.             <remarks>
1060.             You can modify this value to reduce repetitiveness of generated tokens. Similar to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.FrequencyPenalty"/>,
1061.             except that this penalty is applied equally to all tokens that have already appeared, regardless of their exact frequencies.
1062.             </remarks>
1063.         </member>
1064.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Seed">
1065.             <summary>Gets or sets a seed value used by a service to control the reproducibility of results.</summary>
1066.         </member>
1067.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ResponseFormat">
1068.             <summary>
1069.             Gets or sets the response format for the chat request.
1070.             </summary>
1071.             <remarks>
1072.             If <see langword="null"/>, no response format is specified and the client will use its default.
1073.             This property can be set to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat.Text"/> to specify that the response should be unstructured text,
1074.             to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat.Json"/> to specify that the response should be structured JSON data, or
1075.             an instance of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormatJson"/> constructed with a specific JSON schema to request that the
1076.             response be structured JSON data according to that schema. It is up to the client implementation if or how
1077.             to honor the request. If the client implementation doesn't recognize the specific kind of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat"/>,
1078.             it can be ignored.
1079.             </remarks>
1080.         </member>
1081.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ModelId">
1082.             <summary>Gets or sets the model ID for the chat request.</summary>
1083.         </member>
1084.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.StopSequences">
1085.             <summary>
1086.             Gets or sets the list of stop sequences.
1087.             </summary>
1088.             <remarks>
1089.             After a stop sequence is detected, the model stops generating further tokens for chat responses.
1090.             </remarks>
1091.         </member>
1092.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.AllowMultipleToolCalls">
1093.             <summary>
1094.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether a single response is allowed to include multiple tool calls.
1095.             </summary>
1096.             <value>
1097.             <see langword="true"/> for no limit. <see langword="false"/> if the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> is asked to return a maximum of one tool call per request. If <see langword="null"/>, the provider can select its own default.
1098.             </value>
1099.             <remarks>
1100.             <para>
1101.             When used with function calling middleware, this does not affect the ability to perform multiple function calls in sequence.
1102.             It only affects the number of function calls within a single iteration of the function calling loop.
1103.             </para>
1104.             <para>
1105.             The underlying provider is not guaranteed to support or honor this flag. For example it might choose to ignore it and return multiple tool calls regardless.
1106.             </para>
1107.             </remarks>
1108.         </member>
1109.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ToolMode">
1110.             <summary>Gets or sets the tool mode for the chat request.</summary>
1111.             <value>The default is <see langword="null"/>, which is treated the same as <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatToolMode.Auto"/>.</value>
1112.         </member>
1113.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools">
1114.             <summary>Gets or sets the list of tools to include with a chat request.</summary>
1115.             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#tool-calling">Tool calling.</related>
1116.         </member>
1117.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses">
1118.             <summary>Gets or sets a value indicating whether the background responses are allowed.</summary>
1119.             <remarks>
1120.             <para>
1121.             Background responses allow running long-running operations or tasks asynchronously in the background that can be resumed by streaming APIs
1122.             and polled for completion by non-streaming APIs.
1123.             </para>
1124.             <para>
1125.             When this property is set to <see langword="true" />, non-streaming APIs have permission to start a background operation and return an initial
1126.             response with a continuation token. Subsequent calls to the same API should be made in a polling manner with
1127.             the continuation token to get the final result of the operation.
1128.             </para>
1129.             <para>
1130.             When this property is set to <see langword="true" />, streaming APIs are also permitted to start a background operation and begin streaming
1131.             response updates until the operation is completed. If the streaming connection is interrupted, the
1132.             continuation token obtained from the last update that has one should be supplied to a subsequent call to the same streaming API
1133.             to resume the stream from the point of interruption and continue receiving updates until the operation is completed.
1134.             </para>
1135.             <para>
1136.             This property only takes effect if the implementation it's used with supports background responses.
1137.             If the implementation does not support background responses, this property will be ignored.
1138.             </para>
1139.             </remarks>
1140.         </member>
1141.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ContinuationToken">
1142.             <summary>Gets or sets the continuation token for resuming and getting the result of the chat response identified by this token.</summary>
1143.             <remarks>
1144.             This property is used for background responses that can be activated via the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses"/>
1145.             property if the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> implementation supports them.
1146.             Streamed background responses, such as those returned by default by <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>,
1147.             can be resumed if interrupted. This means that a continuation token obtained from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseUpdate.ContinuationToken"/>
1148.             of an update just before the interruption occurred can be passed to this property to resume the stream from the point of interruption.
1149.             Non-streamed background responses, such as those returned by <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>,
1150.             can be polled for completion by obtaining the token from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponse.ContinuationToken"/> property

            <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#tool-calling">Tool calling.</related>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses">
            <summary>Gets or sets a value indicating whether the background responses are allowed.</summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            Background responses allow running long-running operations or tasks asynchronously in the background that can be resumed by streaming APIs
            and polled for completion by non-streaming APIs.
            </para>
--
            The clone will have the same values for all properties as the original instance. Any collections, like <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools"/>,
            <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.StopSequences"/>, and <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.AdditionalProperties"/>, are shallow-cloned, meaning a new collection instance is created,
            but any references contained by the collections are shared with the original.
---
            An individual request may override this value via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ModelId"/>.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatFinishReason">
            <summary>Represents the reason a chat response completed.</summary>
        </member>
--
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ModelId">
            <summary>Gets or sets the model ID for the chat request.</summary>
        </member>
<exited with exit code 0>

kind.prompt:
I need to understand the APIs available in these .NET NuGet packages to build an AI agent with tool calling:

1. Microsoft.Extensions.AI (v9.10.2) - at /home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai/9.10.2/
2. Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions (v9.10.2) - at /home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.abstractions/9.10.2/
3. Microsoft.Agents.AI (v1.3.0) - at /home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.3.0/
4. Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI (v9.*) - at /home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.openai/

For each package, look at the XML docs (*.xml files) in the lib/ directories and README files. I need to understand:
- How to create an IChatClient
- How to define tools (AIFunction / AITool)  
- How the agent loop works (does it auto-invoke tools or do I need manual loop?)
- How to use ChatOptions with tools
- Key classes and their namespaces

Decompile or examine the DLLs if needed. Also check if there are any README.md or docs in the package directories.

Focus especially on: FunctionInvokingChatClient, ChatClientBuilder, AIFunctionFactory, McpToolsAIFunction, or similar tool-related classes.

data.content:
Everything works. Here's what was built:

**Research Agent** — a .NET 10 console app with 4 tools available to the AI agent:

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `SearchWeb` | Queries DuckDuckGo and returns result snippets with URLs |
| `FetchPage` | Fetches and extracts readable text from a web page |
| `SaveNote` | Records a finding with title, content, and source |
| `ReadNotes` | Retrieves all saved notes for review |

**How it works:**
- Uses `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` with `FunctionInvokingChatClient` to automatically handle the tool-calling loop
- The agent follows a structured workflow: search → read pages → save notes → summarize
- Streams the final response to the console in real time

**Usage:**
```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
dotnet run -- "quantum computing breakthroughs 2026"
# or run interactively (prompts for topic)
dotnet run
```

Set `OPENAI_MODEL` to override the default `gpt-4o-mini`.
